title: CITE 201 -- Programming 1 | Midterm Examination
inst: Read all questions carefully before answering. Write your answers legibly. Erasures are not allowed -- use correction fluid only. Strictly NO CHEATING. Any form of dishonesty will result in a grade of ZERO (0) for the entire exam. Total: 100 points.

sections.inst: Circle the letter of the best answer. Each item is worth 2 points.

qts.txt: Which of the following is NOT a valid data type in C#?
What keyword is used to declare a constant in C#?
What is the output of the following code? Console.WriteLine(10 % 3);
Which control structure is used to execute a block of code a fixed number of times?
What does OOP stand for?
Which access modifier makes a class member accessible only within the same class?
In C#, which symbol is used to access a member of an object?
What is the default value of an int variable in C#?
Which of the following is the correct way to declare a single-dimensional array in C#?
What is the purpose of the "return" statement inside a method?
chs: C. It sends a value back to the caller

sections.inst: Write TRUE if the statement is correct and FALSE if it is incorrect on the blank before each number. Each item is worth 1 point.

qts.txt: A string in C# is a reference type.
The "void" return type means a method returns zero.
An array index in C# starts at 1.
Encapsulation is one of the four pillars of OOP.
The "break" statement can be used to exit a loop prematurely.
Inheritance allows a child class to use the properties and methods of a parent class.
A constructor must have the same name as the class.
The "==" operator compares values while ".Equals()" compares references for strings.
A "for" loop always requires an initialization, condition, and increment.
In C#, you can have multiple catch blocks for a single try block.

sections.sec: Test III: Fill in the Blanks
sections.inst: Fill in each blank with the correct word or phrase to complete the statement. Each correct answer is worth 1 point. Spelling must be exact.

qts.txt: The process of hiding implementation details and exposing only necessary parts is called _____________________.
A _____________________ is a blueprint for creating objects in OOP.
The C# keyword _____________________ is used to create a new instance of a class.
An if-else-if chain can be replaced with a _____________________ statement for cleaner code.
The _____________________ loop checks its condition AFTER executing its body at least once.
In C#, the _____________________ keyword is used to define a method that can be overridden in a subclass.
A method that has the same name as the class and no return type is called a _____________________.
The act of a subclass providing its own implementation of an inherited method is called _____________________.
The _____________________ property of an array returns the total number of elements it contains.
An exception that is not handled will cause the program to _____________________ abnormally.

sections.inst: Match Column A with the correct term/description in Column B. Write only the letter of your answer on the space provided. Each correct match is worth 1 point. Each letter in Column B may be used only ONCE.

qts.txt: Defines the blueprint of an object
Prevents inheritance of a class
The ability of objects to take many forms
A method without a body (no implementation)
Represents a single character value
Used to handle runtime errors
Inheriting from more than one class indirectly
A named constant block of statements that returns a value
The operator used to check if an object is null-safe in C#
Converts one data type to another explicitly
A variable that belongs to the class, not instances
A class that cannot be instantiated directly
Calling the parent constructor from a child class
An interface member must be _____ in its implementing class
Used to define a group of named integer constants

sections.sec: Test V: Hand Tracing / Code Analysis
sections.inst: Trace the given code and write the exact output on the space provided. Show your trace work for partial credit. Each item is worth 4 points.

qts.txt: int x = 5, y = 3; int result = x * y + (x - y) * 2; Console.WriteLine("Result: " + result); for (int i = 1; i <= 3; i++) { Console.WriteLine(i + " x " + x + " = " + (i * x)); }

string[] fruits = {"Mango", "Banana", "Pineapple"}; foreach (string fruit in fruits) { if (fruit.Length > 5) Console.WriteLine(fruit + " has more than 5 letters."); else Console.WriteLine(fruit + " is short."); }
qts.ans:
Mango has more than 5 letters.
Banana has more than 5 letters.
Pineapple has more than 5 letters.

qts.txt: int n = 10; int sum = 0; while (n > 0) { sum += n; n -= 3; Console.WriteLine("n = " + n + ", sum = " + sum); }
static int Factorial(int n) { if (n == 0 || n == 1) return 1; return n * Factorial(n - 1); } Console.WriteLine(Factorial(4));
class Animal { public virtual void Sound() { Console.WriteLine("..."); } } class Dog : Animal { public override void Sound() { Console.WriteLine("Woof!"); } } Animal a = new Dog(); a.Sound();

sections.inst: Write a complete and working C# console program for each problem. Follow proper naming conventions, indentation, and syntax. Partial credit may be given for correct logic even with minor syntax errors. Each item is worth 5 points.

qts.txt: Write a C# program that asks the user to input 5 integers and displays their sum, average, highest value, and lowest value.
qts.rub: Correct logic and algorithm (2 pts), Correct class/method structure (1 pt), Correct input/output handling (1 pt), Proper indentation (1 pt)

qts.txt: Create a class called "Student" with the following attributes: name (string), studentNumber (string), and GPA (double). Include a constructor, appropriate getters and setters, and a method called DisplayInfo() that prints all student information in a formatted manner. Instantiate the class in the Main method and call DisplayInfo().
qts.rub: Correct logic and algorithm (2 pts), Correct class/method structure (1 pt), Correct input/output handling (1 pt), Proper indentation (1 pt)

qts.txt: Write a C# program that reads 10 integers from the user, stores them in an array, and uses a method called BubbleSort() to sort the array in ascending order. Display the unsorted and sorted arrays.
qts.rub: Correct logic and algorithm (2 pts), Correct class/method structure (1 pt), Correct input/output handling (1 pt), Proper indentation (1 pt)

sections.inst: Answer the following questions in 3 to 5 sentences each. Be concise, clear, and use proper technical terminology. Each item is worth 5 points.

qts.txt: Explain the concept of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and briefly describe each of its four fundamental principles: Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, and Polymorphism. Give a real-world analogy for any one of the four principles.
qts.rub: Accuracy and completeness of technical content (2 pts), Clarity and organization of explanation (1 pt), Correct use of technical terms and vocabulary (1 pt), Quality of real-world analogy or example given (1 pt)

qts.txt: Differentiate between a "for" loop, a "while" loop, and a "do-while" loop in C#. Describe a specific scenario or use case where each type of loop is the most appropriate choice, and explain why.
qts.rub: Accuracy and completeness of technical content (2 pts), Clarity and organization of explanation (1 pt), Correct use of technical terms and vocabulary (1 pt), Quality of real-world analogy or example given (1 pt)
